2009-05-01.title: Training Afghans as Bullets Fly
2009-05-01.abstract: A firebase, where two young Marines train Afghan soldiers, offers a fine-grained glimpse inside the war.

2009-05-01.title: Iranians and Others Outwit Net Censors
2009-05-01.abstract: Computers are becoming more crucial in global conflicts, not only in spying and military action, but also in determining what information reaches people.

2009-05-01.title: W.H.O. Gives Virus a Name That’s More Scientific and Less Loaded
2009-05-01.abstract: A spokesman for the World Health Organization, which began referring to swine influenza as influenza A(H1N1), said the original name had led to fears about eating pork.

2009-05-01.title: Report Says World Bank’s AIDS Efforts Are Failing
2009-05-01.abstract: An assessment finds that complex AIDS projects have not worked adequately, with the ones in Africa, the region at registering the worst record.

2009-05-01.title: Virus’s Tangled Genes Straddle Continents, Raising a Mystery About Its Origins
2009-05-01.abstract: Swine flu appears to have a combination of genes from two normally separate sets of pigs, those from the Americas and from Eurasia, scientists say.

2009-05-02.title: Ex-Spy Sits Down With Islamists and the West
2009-05-02.abstract: Alastair Crooke, who worked for the British secret intelligence service, has been organizing confidential meetings between Western diplomats and Islamists for several years.

2009-05-02.title: In Pakistan, U.S. Courts Leader of Opposition
2009-05-02.abstract: The Obama administration is reaching out to the former prime minister, Nawaz Sharif, whose ties to Islamists could help President Asif Ali Zardari confront the Taliban.

2009-05-02.title: Turkey: Erdogan Shakes Up Cabinet
2009-05-02.abstract: Ahmet Davutoglu, the official who has shifted Turkish foreign policy toward a greater focus on the Middle East, was made foreign minister.

2009-05-02.title: The Netherlands: Driver of Car in Attack Dies
2009-05-02.abstract: The driver of a car that slammed into a crowd in the Netherlands, killing six people and narrowly missing an open-topped bus carrying Queen Beatrix and other members of the royal family, has died.

2009-05-02.title: Can’t Get Midwife? YouTube Will Assist
2009-05-02.abstract: A father applied lessons learned from his Web search on “how to deliver a baby” to help his wife through labor.

2009-05-03.title: Visual Diary: Fashion
2009-05-03.abstract: All dressed up for a class field trip to one of Saddam’s former palaces.

2009-05-03.title: Plea for Cease-Fire Help in Sri Lanka
2009-05-03.abstract: The Tamil Tigers, besieged and surrounded, asked Britain and France to press the government for a cease-fire.

2009-05-03.title: Flu, Mostly Mild, Has Spread Across U.S.
2009-05-03.abstract: Health officials said there were 226 cases of swine flu in 30 states, with more expected to turn up in additional states in the next few days.

2009-05-03.title: Italy Premier’s Drama Unfolds in Press
2009-05-03.abstract: Less than a week after writing an open letter criticizing her husband for cavorting with much younger women, the wife of Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi said she wanted to file for divorce.

2009-05-03.title: Pakistan Says Islamic Court Fulfills Deal With Taliban
2009-05-03.abstract: Having created an Islamic appeals court, the Pakistani government said the Taliban should cease hostilities.

2009-05-04.title: Senators Accuse Pentagon of Delay in Recovering Millions
2009-05-04.abstract: The Pentagon has done little to collect at least $100 million in overcharges from the defense contractor KBR, two senators said.

2009-05-04.title: Israel Faces a Hard Sell in Bid to Shift Policy
2009-05-04.abstract: The new government wants to reorient Israeli foreign policy away from a prospective Palestinian state and toward the rising threat from Iran.

2009-05-04.title: Predicting Flu With the Aid of (George) Washington
2009-05-04.abstract: The routes taken by millions of dollar bills are at the core of a computer model that is predicting the swine flu epidemic’s future.

2009-05-04.title: Pakistan Strife Raises U.S. Doubt on Nuclear Arms
2009-05-04.abstract: Fears about control of a weapons arsenal increase as an insurgency by the Taliban and Al Qaeda expands its reach.

2009-05-04.title: Salamo Arouch, Who Boxed for His Life in Auschwitz, Is Dead at 86
2009-05-04.abstract: The Greek-born Jewish boxer, who survived by winning fights against fellow prisoners to the delight of Nazi guards, was the basis of the 1989 movie “Triumph of the Spirit.”

2009-05-05.title: Debate on Trump Project Takes the Low Road
2009-05-05.abstract: Opponents of a $1.5 billion development in Scotland are learning that in hard times, few people are sympathetic to environmental concerns.

2009-05-05.title: Indonesia: Official Suspected of Murder
2009-05-05.abstract: Local news media reported that the leader of the Corruption Eradication Commission and the victim had both been involved with a 22-year-old female golf caddy.

2009-05-05.title: Egypt: Gates Seeks to Assure Allies
2009-05-05.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates landed in Cairo on a tour of the Middle East intended to reassure Egypt and Saudi Arabia that they remain valued partners.

2009-05-05.title: French Are No. 1 in Eating and Sleeping
2009-05-05.abstract: French people spend more time eating and sleeping than the habitants of any other highly developed country, according to a report published Monday.

2009-05-05.title: Italy: Warning on Iran by Israeli Official
2009-05-05.abstract: Beginning his first trip abroad as Israel’s foreign minister, Avigdor Lieberman said in Rome that Iran posed the “greatest threat” to the Middle East.

2009-05-06.title: Back to Hell?
2009-05-06.abstract: They could lose everything they have gained so far because it seems the Iraqis have put their feet on the road to sectarian war, and our experiment has revealed that walking on this road is easier and faster than walking on the road of reconciliation.

2009-05-06.title: Italian Bishops Criticize Berlusconi
2009-05-06.abstract: “It’s worrisome that politics and spectacle, caught in a death grip, have brought out the worst in each other,” a newspaper published by the Italian Bishops Conference wrote in a front-page editorial.

2009-05-06.title: Iraqi Report on Corruption Cites Prosecutors’ Barriers
2009-05-06.abstract: Cases include embezzlement of $80 million, tampering with government tea imports, the theft of 50 Italian-made Beretta pistols and procuring a forged Ph.D.

2009-05-06.title: High Court in Germany Pops Names That Balloon
2009-05-06.abstract: The German Constitutional Court upheld a ban on married people combining already-hyphenated names, forbidding last names of three parts or more.

2009-05-06.title: Amid France’s Woes, Satirists Turn to Sarkozy, as a Punch Line
2009-05-06.abstract: Comedians’ growing boldness is a sign of unhappiness with the government as it struggles with rising unemployment and worker anger.

2009-05-07.title: Ambush by an Ally Chills Trust in Iraqi Units
2009-05-07.abstract: An Iraqi soldier killed Master Sgt. Anthony Davis, an American embedded in his battalion. In six months, seven soldiers have been killed by Iraqis in uniform.

2009-05-07.title: The Slaying of Master Sergeant Davis
2009-05-07.abstract: A deadly attack by an Iraqi in uniform shatters the trust between a small team of American military trainers and their Iraqi trainees in one remote northern town.

2009-05-07.title: Debating the Wisdom of ‘Swine Flu Parties’
2009-05-07.abstract: Most experts say that deliberately seeking out the H1N1 virus in the hopes of becoming immunized is a bad idea.

2009-05-07.title: Britain: Libya Seeks Inmate’s Transfer
2009-05-07.abstract: Libya has made a formal request of Britain for the transfer to a Libyan jail of Abdel Basset Ali al-Megrahi, the only person to have been convicted in the bombing of Pan Am Flight 103.

2009-05-07.title: Gaza Militants and Israel Exchange Fire
2009-05-07.abstract: Militants in Gaza fired mortar shells into a field in southern Israel, and Israel retaliated with airstrikes on three smuggling tunnels along Gaza’s border with Egypt, the Israeli military said.

2009-05-08.title: Flu Is Injecting Itself Into Mexican Politics
2009-05-08.abstract: With midterm elections scheduled for July, the A (H1N1) virus has replaced the slumping economy and the drug war as the top worry for voters.

2009-05-08.title: Myanmar: U.S. Man Held in Swim to Dissident’s Home
2009-05-08.abstract: An American man has been arrested for swimming across a lake to the home of the Nobel Peace Prize laureate Daw Aung San Suu Kyi.

2009-05-08.title: China: Monk Freed After 6 Months in Prison
2009-05-08.abstract: The monk was detained by security officers after he appeared in a video on YouTube in which he described being tortured during another period of detention.

2009-05-08.title: Corrections
2009-05-08.abstract: An article on Thursday about the young age of people who have developed serious swine flu complications misstated the number of vaccine doses that drug makers could produce after receiving a seed vaccine, according to World Health Organization estimates. It is 1 billion to 2 billion doses in a year, not 1.2 billion in six months. The error also appeared in an article on Thursday about the idea of trying to become infected with the virus now to provide some immunity in case swine flu comes back stronger later.

2009-05-08.title: Somalia's Pirates Face Growing Backlash
2009-05-08.abstract: Abshir Boyah, a pirate who says he has hijacked more than 25 ships off the coast of Somalia, says he will give up this career if certain terms are met.

2009-05-09.title: Adroit Envoy States Case for Pakistan
2009-05-09.abstract: Husain Haqqani, Pakistan’s ambassador to the U.S. and a lucid interpreter of his country’s politics, has become influential in Washington.

2009-05-09.title: General Sees a Longer Stay in Iraq Cities for U.S. Troops
2009-05-09.abstract: Gen. Ray Odierno said one-fifth of American combat troops would stay behind in Iraqi cities even after the June 30 deadline set for the departure.

2009-05-09.title: Obama to Speak From Egypt in Address to Muslim World
2009-05-09.abstract: White House officials would not say exactly where in Egypt the president would give his highly anticipated June 4 speech.

2009-05-09.title: Karzai’s Ex-Allies Vie for Afghan Presidency
2009-05-09.abstract: Afghanistan’s president has seen his popularity wane, but he is still regarded as the leading candidate among the 44 who have submitted nominations.

2009-05-09.title: Mexico: Cuba Trip May Be Delayed
2009-05-09.abstract: President Felipe Calderón said that he might postpone a planned trip because of a decision by the Cuban government, in the wake of the swine flu outbreak, to ban flights between the countries.

2009-05-10.title: The Caucus: Gen. Odierno’s Facebook
2009-05-10.abstract: The Caucus: General Ray Odierno’s Facebook page

2009-05-10.title: Ethnic Groups in Myanmar Hope for Peace, but Gird for Fight
2009-05-10.abstract: As the military government prepares to adopt a new Constitution, cease-fires with armed ethnic groups are fraying.

2009-05-10.title: Pope Celebrates Mass in Jordan
2009-05-10.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI celebrated Mass, encouraging Christians in the Middle East to stay faithful to their roots to “build new bridges” to fight extremism.

2009-05-10.title: The Brawn Pit Stop Strategy
2009-05-10.abstract: Although it is a topic that I have inevitably written about in my race report for today’s Spanish Grand Prix, I feel an equal desire to write about it here – partly because I’m sure it’s a topic that people will be burning to discuss.

2009-05-10.title: Doctor Reports 378 Dead in Sri Lanka
2009-05-10.abstract: A government doctor in Sri Lanka’s war zone said that 378 civilians were killed and more than 1,100 were wounded during intensive shelling.

2009-05-11.title: Putin Says NATO Exercises in Georgia Hinder U.S.-Russia Relations
2009-05-11.abstract: The Russian prime minister said Sunday that he hoped the United States would “step on the brake hard” to prevent the relationship from deteriorating.

2009-05-11.title: Venetia Phair Dies at 90; as a Girl, She Named Pluto
2009-05-11.abstract: As an 11-year-old in Britain, Mrs. Phair suggested the name Pluto to her well-connected grandfather.

2009-05-11.title: New South African Leader Emphasizes Continuity in Cabinet Lineup
2009-05-11.abstract: Jacob Zuma’s appointments largely signaled a desire for continuity rather than any lunge to the left, as some had hoped and others feared.

2009-05-11.title: 7 Are Killed in Suicide Bombings in Afghanistan
2009-05-11.abstract: It was the second attack in four days in Gereshk, a town in the most lawless of the country’s provinces, Helmand.

2009-05-11.title: Shaky Pakistan Is Seen as Target of Qaeda Plots
2009-05-11.abstract: Foreign operatives of Al Qaeda are moving to sow chaos in Pakistan and strengthen the hand of the militant Islamist groups there, U.S. and Pakistani intelligence officials say.

2009-05-12.title: Egypt to Be Center Stage in Obama’s Address to Arabs
2009-05-12.abstract: President Obama’s decision to deliver a speech to the Muslim world from Egypt is fraught with potential perils.

2009-05-12.title: French Find Safety Nets Multiplying in Pastures
2009-05-12.abstract: In these anxious days, some people are putting their cash into cows, hoping for a safe investment that allows for long-term growth from a renewable resource: calves.

2009-05-12.abstract: From changing the lights to buying pornography and horse manure, expenses charged to the public have stirred fury.

2009-05-12.title: Man Accused of Crimes at a Nazi Camp Is Deported
2009-05-12.abstract: John Demjanjuk, 89, will stand trial in Germany, where he is accused of being an accessory in the murder of 29,000 Jews.

2009-05-12.title: Zimbabwe: Newspaper Editors Are Arrested
2009-05-12.abstract: The charges are related to an article naming that named police officials and intelligence agents that it said were involved in the abduction of activists last year.

2009-05-13.title: Mideast’s Christians Losing Numbers and Sway
2009-05-13.abstract: The pope this week is addressing a dwindling Christian population driven away by violence, lack of economic opportunity and the rise of radical Islam.

2009-05-13.title: U.S. Joins Rights Panel After a Vote at the U.N.
2009-05-13.abstract: Controversy has surrounded the United Nations Human Rights Council, which has members that are condemned by human rights organizations.

2009-05-13.abstract: The choice of a new breed of general to take over the fight in Afghanistan may mean that thinking about the border with Pakistan has begun to change.

2009-05-13.title: A General Steps From the Shadows
2009-05-13.abstract: Lt. Gen. Stanley A. McChrystal, set to become the top U.S. commander in Afghanistan, is said to move easily between the political and military realms.

2009-05-13.title: Corrections
2009-05-13.abstract: An article on April 27 about the mayoral election in the 2014 Winter Olympic city of Sochi, Russia, which has a large Armenian population, paraphrased incorrectly from remarks about Turkey’s World War I genocide of Armenians made by Garry Kasparov, the former world chess champion, who was visiting Sochi to support the challenger to the Kremlin-backed incumbent. Mr. Kasparov said the “newly born Soviet power” had supported Turkey at the time of the genocide, a reference to the Bolsheviks who were rising to power in Russia at the time. He did not say “Soviet Russia” had supported Turkey. (Soviet Russia, or the Union of Soviet Socialist Republics, was not created until after World War I.).

2009-05-14.title: Lawyer Says Reporter Had Iran Document
2009-05-14.abstract: The case against Roxana Saberi was based on her possession of a classified document and trips she made to Israel.

2009-05-14.title: A Mobster Trial, and a Flash of a Violent Past
2009-05-14.abstract: A leader of one of the country’s most powerful criminal syndicates, Vladimir S. Barsukov once rivaled St. Petersburg’s elected leaders for control over Russia’s imperial capital.

2009-05-14.title: Stalemate in Korangal Valley
2009-05-14.abstract: The American counter-insurgency in a Taliban stronghold in Afghanistan has been at a bloody draw for years now.

2009-05-14.title: Woman in Rendition Case Sues for Immunity
2009-05-14.abstract: A former American official charged with kidnapping in the 2003 seizure of a radical Muslim cleric is seeking diplomatic immunity to halt the prosecution.

2009-05-14.abstract: President Obama cited concerns that the photos of U.S. military personnel abusing prisoners in Iraq and Afghanistan could further antagonize enemies.

2009-05-15.abstract: Oman offers an example of how authoritarian systems eventually rub up against the human desire for freedom and justice.

2009-05-15.abstract: The Obama administration threatened to curb the flow of information if Britain made public the details of interrogation techniques used against a former Guantánamo Bay detainee.

2009-05-15.abstract: Mr. Compagnoni was one of the first two men to reach the summit of K2, the world’s second highest peak and among the most dangerous.

2009-05-15.abstract: Thomas Patrick Gilbert Cholmondeley, the white Kenyan aristocrat convicted last week of killing a black poacher, was given a sentence of eight months in jail.

2009-05-16.abstract: Seeking a monopoly on gas supplies, Gazprom committed to long-term contracts at costs far above current prices.

2009-05-16.abstract: Investigators hope to stop a network of globe-hopping jewel thieves with a taste for chunky diamonds and Swiss watches.

2009-05-16.abstract: Park Dae-sung was South Korea’s most celebrated blogger. His postings on the economy attracted a cultlike following. Then he was arrested and exposed — and his fans turned on him.

2009-05-16.abstract: Delegates from scores of shipping nations signed an agreement to require stronger environmental standards in recycling old ships.

2009-05-16.abstract: Mindful of not antagonizing a powerful neighbor, Oman’s rulers have defied the appeals of others in the Middle East for it to draw away from Tehran.

2009-05-17.title: Men Dressed as Police Free Mexican Inmates
2009-05-17.abstract: Many of those freed, in an operation that took only minutes, were thought to be drug traffickers.

2009-05-17.title: Susanna Agnelli, First Woman in Italy to Run Foreign Office, Dies at 87
2009-05-17.abstract: Ms. Agnelli, a scion of the Fiat car dynasty, was the only female foreign minister in Italian history and a formidable presence who brought American-style philanthropy to Italy.

2009-05-17.title: 2 Studies Tie Disaster Risk to Urban Growth
2009-05-17.abstract: A new U.N. report says that the level of vulnerability does not always mirror economic conditions.

2009-05-17.title: Rebels Routed in Sri Lanka After 25 Years of War
2009-05-17.abstract: The ethnic Tamil separatist rebels of Sri Lanka acknowledged Sunday that their war of over a quarter century for a homeland had “reached its bitter end.”

2009-05-17.title: Vote in India Reshapes Landscape
2009-05-17.abstract: Sonia Gandhi is credited with having scored a stunning political coup for her party, the Indian National Congress.

2009-05-18.title: Little Relief Expected for Flood-Ravaged Brazil
2009-05-18.abstract: With at least 45 dead and 260,000 forced from their homes, local officials are criticizing the government for not doing more.

2009-05-18.abstract: Bahr Idriss Abu Garda, the leader of the United Resistance Front, is accused of participating in the killing of African peacekeepers in 2007.

2009-05-18.abstract: There are new concerns on Capitol Hill about whether billions of dollars in proposed military aid might be diverted to Pakistan’s nuclear program.

2009-05-18.abstract: The daily briefings were called the “Worldwide Intelligence Update” and presented to Secretary of Defense Donald H. Rumsfeld and other officials as they came to work.

2009-05-18.abstract: The authorities declared a final victory in what could be the end of Asia’s longest civil war as the Sinhalese majority celebrates in the streets.

2009-05-19.abstract: For two years, a growing crowd has lined up along the road to show respect whenever a soldier’s hearse drives through town.

2009-05-19.abstract: One of the men, Sheik Riyadh al-Mujami, arrested in Diyala Province, is a prominent figure in the local Awakening Council.

2009-05-19.abstract: Zalmay Khalilzad, a former ambassador to Afghanistan, could assume a powerful position under a plan he is discussing with the Afghan president, officials said.

2009-05-19.title: Pakistan: Fighting Displaced 1.5. Million
2009-05-19.abstract: The displacement crisis is “one of the most dramatic of recent times,” a spokesman for the United Nations refugee agency in Geneva said Monday.

2009-05-19.title: Bill Clinton Expected to Be Haiti Envoy
2009-05-19.abstract: Former President Bill Clinton is expected to be named a special United Nations envoy to Haiti, The Associated Press said Monday.

2009-05-20.title: Arms Sent by U.S. May Be Falling Into Taliban Hands
2009-05-20.abstract: Evidence suggests munitions procured by the Pentagon leak from Afghan forces for use against American troops.

2009-05-20.title: Venezuela’s Hope of More Sway Dims as Riches Dip
2009-05-20.abstract: Hugo Chávez’s socialist agenda is suffering in the downturn as neighbors seek other partnerships.

2009-05-20.title: Crash in Indonesia Kills More Than 90
2009-05-20.abstract: An Indonesian military cargo plane crashed into a village in East Java on Wednesday, killing at least 98 people, air force officials said.

2009-05-20.title: Netanyahu Says He’s Willing to Talk With Syria
2009-05-20.abstract: He says that such negotiations would be without preconditions and that any agreement would have to meet Israel’s security needs.

2009-05-20.abstract: The U.S. military rejected a charge that around 140 noncombatants were killed in a recent bombing in Farah Province, and put the toll instead at 20 to 30 civilians.

2009-05-21.title: U.S. Pullout a Condition in Afghan Peace Talks
2009-05-21.abstract: Leaders of armed groups in Afghanistan are talking to intermediaries about a potential peace agreement.

2009-05-21.title: Witch Hunts and Foul Potions Heighten Fear of Leader in Gambia
2009-05-21.abstract: Dozens, perhaps hundreds, of Gambians are frightened and sick after being rounded up and accused of sorcery. The campaign is the latest exploit of Gambia’s absolute ruler.

2009-05-21.title: Palestinians Try to Prune Branches of Core Party
2009-05-21.abstract: Fatah has been paralyzed by competing personal alliances and a continuing identity crisis, and has not held a congress in 20 years.

2009-05-21.title: Keeping Score on Obama vs. Netanyahu
2009-05-21.abstract: At the early stages of a relationship between the nations’ leaders, their dealings are being analyzed for signs of who has the upper hand.

2009-05-21.title: Serbia Welcomes Biden on Balkan Tour
2009-05-21.abstract: His welcome was a surprising but representative turn of events, in a country where people are increasingly focused on achieving a degree of normality after decades of strife.

2009-05-22.title: At Turkish Border, Armenians Are Wary of a Thaw
2009-05-22.abstract: Armenia and Turkey are outlining a plan to improve relations, but a contentious dispute over the Armenian genocide in World War I is stifling prospects for better ties.

2009-05-22.title: Spread of Swine Flu Puts Japan in Crisis Mode
2009-05-22.abstract: The widening outbreak is a particular shock to this hygiene-obsessed country, which has been harder hit than any other country outside North America.

2009-05-22.title: Guatemalan Leaders Under Pall in Lawyer’s Killing
2009-05-22.abstract: Allegations that the president of Guatemala and others are responsible for the recent killing of a prominent lawyer have thrown the country into a political crisis.

2009-05-22.abstract: The agreement will face resistance in Congress after a video, depicting the torture of an Afghan man by a member of the ruling family of Abu Dhabi, raised concerns about the Emirates.

2009-05-22.title: Britain: Settlement for Gurkhas
2009-05-22.abstract: Britain will ease immigration laws to allow as many as 36,000 Nepalese soldiers who served in the Gurkha units of the British Army before 1997 to settle with their families in Britain.

2009-05-23.title: China: An Encore Performance at Beijing’s Olympic Stadium
2009-05-23.abstract: Zhang Yimou, a prominent Chinese filmmaker, is scheduled return to the Bird’s Nest in Beijing to direct two performances of “Turandot” in October, according to a newspaper report.

2009-05-23.title: Indonesians Embrace American-Style Reality TV
2009-05-23.abstract: Reality shows that mirror American programs and values are growing in popularity as political Islam loses its momentum among voters.

2009-05-23.title: Canadian Released in Terrorism Case
2009-05-23.abstract: A member of a group that a judge said was not particularly competent was freed based on time already served.

2009-05-23.title: W.H.O. to Rewrite Its Pandemic Rules
2009-05-23.abstract: The announcement means that despite the global spread of swine flu, with cases confirmed in 42 counties, it will probably never be declared a full-fledged pandemic.

2009-05-23.title: Roh Moo-hyun, Ex-President of South Korea, Kills Himself
2009-05-23.abstract: Roh Moo-hyun, who had been under criminal investigation for corruption, died after leaping off a cliff, the police said.

2009-05-24.title: Corrections
2009-05-24.abstract: A news analysis article last Sunday about the mixed reviews Pope Benedict XVI received during his trip to the Middle East, including criticism from Israelis over the pope’s failure to acknowledge his past as a youth in Nazi Germany, misstated the publication day of a satirical page in Yediot Aharonot, an Israeli daily newspaper, that poked fun at the criticism. It was Friday, May 15 — not Saturday, May 16. (The newspaper does not publish on Saturday, the Jewish sabbath.).

2009-05-24.title: Pre-Race Notes
2009-05-24.abstract: Despite all the talk of the possibility of rain, at less than an hour before the race in Monaco, the skies look quite blue, just light, hazy cloud cover. Can’t imagine how it could rain. The temperature is 25 degrees Celsius and humidity is at 58 percent. This looks like a dry race – or as the French journalist sitting next to me in the media center said, “There’s a tempest of blue sky….”

2009-05-24.title: Pakistan Says It Makes More Gains on Taliban
2009-05-24.abstract: The army said it had taken the central square of Mingora, the largest city in the Swat Valley.

2009-05-24.title: Monaco’s Underdogs
2009-05-24.abstract: It was a fascinating race today, despite the urge to fall asleep because of the procession. No, the fascination was not so much with the winners of the race and the other “contenders” – which I have reported on in my race report – but on the people farther down the pack.

2009-05-24.title: At Least 22 Killed in Iraq Attacks
2009-05-24.abstract: The eight attacks in Mosul and Fallujah happened on the same day that military spokesmen announced a continued drop in overall violence.

2009-05-25.title: Mongolia Awaits Outcome of Presidential Vote
2009-05-25.abstract: Voters cast their ballots amid worries about the country’s economic future and fears that the results could spark the kind of violence that killed five people last year.

2009-05-25.title: At U.N., a Sandinista’s Plan for Recovery
2009-05-25.abstract: To cure the world’s economic woes, Miguel d’Escoto Brockmann of Nicaragua, the General Assembly president, wants to establish nine new institutions.

2009-05-25.title: Cleaning Cairo, but Taking a Livelihood
2009-05-25.abstract: The zabaleen, garbage collectors who live in neighborhoods spilling over with trash, fear that a huge government pig cull was only the beginning of the end for them.

2009-05-25.title: Targeting Pigs and Christians in Egypt
2009-05-25.abstract: Zabaleen Christians in Cairo react to Egypt's plan to cull the country's pig population in wake of the global swine flu epidemic.

2009-05-25.title: For a City in Gridlock, a Lifeline and a Scourge
2009-05-25.abstract: In an anarchic city, thousands of motorbike couriers known as motorizados make life there treacherous and viable at the same time.

2009-05-26.title: A Job and No Mortgage for All in a Spanish Town
2009-05-26.abstract: The mayor of Marinaleda boasts that his town, with its municipal housing program and bustling farming cooperative, is a Communist oasis that people are flocking to.

2009-05-26.title: In Lebanon’s Patchwork, a Focus on Armenians’ Political Might
2009-05-26.abstract: The main Armenian political bloc, Tashnaq, brought new attention to itself when it decided to support Hezbollah’s alliance in the coming parliamentary elections.

2009-05-26.title: Nomination of U.S. Afghan Commander Revives Questions in Tillman Case
2009-05-26.abstract: In inquiries after the “friendly fire” death of a former football star, Lt. Gen. Stanley A. McChrystal was criticized but then exonerated.

2009-05-26.title: Politician’s Novel Idea for Mexican Tourism: Statue of Swine Flu Survivor
2009-05-26.abstract: Gov. Fidel Herrera of Veracruz suggested the tribute to Édgar Hernández, 5, whom he calls the world’s first H1N1 survivor.

2009-05-26.title: Russian Uranium Sale to U.S. Is Planned
2009-05-26.abstract: With the signing of a contract, Russia’s nuclear-fuel trade with the United States will shift to a purely commercial footing.

2009-05-27.title: Mexican Sweep Nets Officials
2009-05-27.abstract: A large-scale antidrug operation resulted in the arrests of 10 mayors and nearly two dozen other government and police officials, the authorities said.

2009-05-27.title: Ex-Detainee Describes His 7 Years at Guantánamo
2009-05-27.abstract: An Algerian given a new home in France says he was interrogated for 16 straight nights in 2003.

2009-05-27.title: In Siberia, the Death Knell of a Complex Holding a Deadly Stockpile
2009-05-27.abstract: A 1994 inspection in Shchuchye was a catalyst for an American plan to destroy artillery shells and warheads filled with nerve agents that were found there.

2009-05-27.title: Georgian Opposition Takes Railroad Station
2009-05-27.abstract: The move represented an escalation of the opposition’s tactics, and indicated that its leaders were now trying to put economic pressure on the president.

2009-05-27.title: Leadership Mystery Amid North Korea’s Nuclear Work
2009-05-27.abstract: Political uncertainty and Kim Jong-il’s questionable health are two of the issues that contribute to a diplomatic challenge facing the Obama administration.

2009-05-28.title: Modern Dance and Art Bring a Burst of Color to a Gray City
2009-05-28.abstract: Curators from Moscow who have embraced this military-industrial city see the setting as reverting to a cultural birthplace.

2009-05-28.title: Inquiry Into Oil Company May Hurt Brazil’s Fiscal Plan
2009-05-28.abstract: The investigation of Petrobas will complicate efforts to wring revenue from the deepwater oil fields that are expected to make the country a global energy power.

2009-05-28.title: South Koreans Express Fatigue With a Recalcitrant North
2009-05-28.abstract: After years of hope that relations with the North would thaw if the South tried to coax it into engagement, regional experts and others speak of growing disenchantment.

2009-05-28.title: Georgia: Rail Station Protest Breaks Up
2009-05-28.abstract: Opponents of Georgia’s president, Mikheil Saakashvili, ended a protest on Wednesday at the main railroad station in the capital, Tbilisi.

2009-05-28.title: Argentina: Inquiry Into Bombing to Reopen
2009-05-28.abstract: Argentina’s Supreme Court ordered the reopening of the investigation into a deadly 1994 bombing of a Jewish community center in Buenos Aires.

2009-05-29.title: Candidacy for Unesco by Egyptian Is Faltering
2009-05-29.abstract: Farouk Hosny, Egypt’s culture minister, apologized for inflammatory remarks about Israel, but critics have urged blocking his candidacy for the United Nations cultural agency.

2009-05-29.title: In Reporting a Scandal, the Media Are Accused of Just Listening
2009-05-29.abstract: A highly publicized corruption investigation has weakened Japan’s opposition party, but many worry about the failure of the news media to press the prosecutors for answers.

2009-05-29.title: Gates to Reassure Allies in Asia Over North Korea
2009-05-29.abstract: Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates said that the United States had detected no unusual military movement in North Korea, and had no plans to reinforce American forces in South Korea.

2009-05-29.title: The Rev. Gérard Jean-Juste, Champion of Haitian Rights in U.S., Dies at 62
2009-05-29.abstract: Father Jean-Juste came to prominence in the late 1970s as director of the Haitian Refugee Center in Miami, where he became a vocal advocate of Haitians seeking asylum in the United States.

2009-05-29.title: Prime Minister’s Escapades Finally Raise Eyebrows
2009-05-29.abstract: The debate over Silvio Berlusconi is not just about sex, but critics say, reflects an inattention to Italy’s deep problems.

2009-05-30.title: Honduras: Report on Attacks on Transgender People
2009-05-30.abstract: Violence against transgender people is rarely investigated by the Honduran authorities, Human Rights Watch said Friday.

2009-05-30.title: North Korea Is Warned by Gates on Testing
2009-05-30.abstract: The defense secretary warned North Korea that the U.S. will not accept it as a nuclear-armed state.

2009-05-30.title: Chávez Seeks Tighter Grip on Military
2009-05-30.abstract: The efforts of President Hugo Chávez of Venezuela to strengthen his hold on the armed forces have led to high-profile arrests and a wave of reassignments.

2009-05-30.title: Pakistan Army Claims Control of Main Swat Town
2009-05-30.abstract: A general says the public helped win a major victory in a three-week campaign against the Taliban.

2009-05-30.title: Iranian Candidate Taps Student Woes
2009-05-30.abstract: Mir Hussein Moussavi, a moderate politician, is the strongest challenger to Iran’s president, Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, ahead of the June 12 election.

2009-05-31.title: Indications of Another Missile Test
2009-05-31.abstract: The possible shipment of an intercontinental ballistic missile could be a sign that North Korea might be planning another long-range missile test.

2009-05-31.title: Gates Looks to Tougher Approach on North Korea
2009-05-31.abstract: The U.S. defense secretary raised the idea of a tougher approach toward North Korea in meetings with Asian allies at the annual security conference in Singapore, officials said.

2009-05-31.title: Visual Diary: A Slide Revisited
2009-05-31.abstract: A boy on a slide may be a good metaphor for the mood in Iraq.

2009-05-31.title: Gates Grounded in Singapore
2009-05-31.abstract: The specially fitted 747 that Defense Secretary Robert M. Gates uses for travel broke down, and instead of traveling to Manila, he spent another night in Singapore.

2009-05-31.title: 6 Die as Palestinian Authority Forces Clash With Hamas
2009-05-31.abstract: Palestinian Authority forces clashed with Hamas militants, leaving six dead in the bloodiest such encounter in two years.
